sections.content:
3 cells changed. Player center is now c, target center is W. The level is still not complete. I've been going in circles with the swap mechanic. Let me fundamentally reconsider what the goal might be.
Let me look at the board holistically:
- Left panel: white background, charcoal frame with green interior (when player is inside)
- Right panel: light gray background, charcoal frame with light gray interior, target green frame
- Purple bar in the middle
- Gray bridge connecting left and right panels
Maybe the goal is NOT about color matching. Maybe it's about:
1. Moving the player to a specific position
2. Clicking on a specific pixel to complete the level
3. Some combination of movement and clicking
Let me try clicking on different parts of the board. I've tried:
- Target center (when c): swaps colors
- Target center (when W): does nothing
- Charcoal frame interior: swaps colors
- Charcoal frame border: swaps colors
What I haven't tried:
- Clicking on the purple bar
- Clicking on the gray bridge
- Clicking on the background (white or light gray areas)
- Clicking on the target's green frame border
- Clicking on the player's green frame border
Let me try clicking on the purple bar.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state
after = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']

print("=== All objects ===")
for n in after:
    b = n['boundary']
    r_min = min(p[0] for p in b)
    c_min = min(p[1] for p in b)
    r_max = max(p[0] for p in b)
    c_max = max(p[1] for p in b)
    print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, bbox=[{r_min},{c_min}]-[{r_max},{c_max}]")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
